title: Scikit-learn: Machine Learning in Python
abstract: Scikit-learn is a Python module integrating wide range of state-of-the-art machine learning algorithms for medium-scale supervised and unsupervised problems. This package focuses on bringing to non-specialists using general-purpose high-level language. Emphasis put ease use, performance, documentation, API consistency. It has minimal dependencies distributed under the simplified BSD license, encouraging its use in both academic commercial settings. Source code, binaries, documentation can be downloaded from http://scikit-learn.org.

title: Convolutional LSTM Network: A Machine Learning Approach for Precipitation Nowcasting
abstract: The goal of precipitation nowcasting is to predict the future rainfall intensity in a local region over relatively short period time. In paper, we formulate as spatiotemporal sequence which both input prediction target are sequences. By extending fully connected LSTM (FC-LSTM) convolutional structures input-to-state state-to-state transitions, propose (ConvLSTM) use it build an end-to-end trainable model for problem. Experiments show that our ConvLSTM network captures correlations better consistently outperforms FC-LSTM state-of-the-art operational ROVER algorithm nowcasting.

title: Predictive Analytics for Readmission of Patients with Congestive Heart Failure
abstract: Mitigating preventable readmissions, where patients are readmitted for the same primary diagnosis within 30 days, poses a significant challenge to delivery of high-quality healthcare. abstract: The popular Q-learning algorithm is known to overestimate action values under certain conditions. title: Rainbow: Combining Improvements in Deep Reinforcement Learning
abstract: The deep reinforcement learning community has made several independent improvements to the DQN algorithm. However, it is unclear which of these extensions are complementary and can be fruitfully combined. This paper examines six algorithm empirically studies their combination. Our experiments show that combination provides state-of-the-art performance on Atari 2600 benchmark, both in terms data efficiency final performance. We also provide results from a detailed ablation study shows contribution each component overall

title: Reinforcement learning and adaptive dynamic programming for feedback control
abstract: Living organisms learn by acting on their environment, observing the resulting reward stimulus, and adjusting actions accordingly to improve reward. This action-based or reinforcement learning can capture notions of optimal behavior occurring in natural systems. We describe mathematical formulations for a practical implementation method known as adaptive dynamic programming. These give us insight into design controllers man-made engineered systems that both exhibit behavior.

abstract: In recent years, neural networks have enjoyed a renaissance as function approximators in reinforcement learning. First, we propose two activation functions for network approximation learning: sigmoid-weighted linear unit (SiLU) and its derivative (dSiLU). SiLU computed by sigmoid multiplied input. Second, suggest that more traditional approach using on-policy with eligibility traces, instead experience replay, softmax action selection can be competitive DQN, without need separate target network. title: Deep reinforcement learning for robotic manipulation with asynchronous off-policy updates
abstract: Reinforcement learning holds the promise of enabling autonomous robots to learn large repertoires behavioral skills with minimal human intervention. However, robotic applications reinforcement often compromise autonomy process in favor achieving training times that are practical for real physical systems. This typically involves introducing hand-engineered policy representations and human-supplied demonstrations. Deep alleviates this limitation by general-purpose neural network policies, but direct deep algorithms have so far been restricted simulated settings relatively simple tasks, due their apparent high sample complexity. In paper, we demonstrate a recent algorithm based on off-policy Q-functions can scale complex 3D manipulation tasks policies efficiently enough train robots. We be further reduced parallelizing across multiple which pool updates asynchronously. Our experimental evaluation shows our method variety simulation door opening skill without any prior demonstrations or manually designed representations.

title: Resource Management with Deep Reinforcement Learning
abstract: Resource management problems in systems and networking often manifest as difficult online decision making tasks where appropriate solutions depend on understanding the workload environment. Inspired by recent advances deep reinforcement learning for AI problems, we consider building that learn to manage resources directly from experience. We present DeepRM, an example solution translates problem of packing with multiple resource demands into a problem. Our initial results show DeepRM performs comparably state-of-the-art heuristics, adapts different conditions, converges quickly, learns strategies are sensible hindsight.

abstract: The authors examine learning in all experiments they could locate involving one hundred periods or more of games with a unique equilibrium mixed strategies, and new experiment. They study both the ex post ('best fit') descriptive power models, their ante predictive power, by simulating each experiment using parameters estimated from other experiments. title: Benchmarking Deep Reinforcement Learning for Continuous Control
abstract: Recently, researchers have made significant progress combining the advances in deep learning for feature representations with reinforcement learning. Some notable examples include training agents to play Atari games based on raw pixel data and acquire advanced manipulation skills using sensory inputs. However, it has been difficult quantify domain of continuous control due lack a commonly adopted benchmark. In this work, we present benchmark suite tasks, including classic tasks like cart-pole swing-up, very high state action dimensionality such as 3D humanoid locomotion, partial observations, hierarchical structure. We report novel findings systematic evaluation range implemented algorithms. abstract: This paper investigates the spectrum sharing problem in vehicular networks based on multi-agent reinforcement learning, where multiple vehicle-to-vehicle (V2V) links reuse frequency preoccupied by vehicle-to-infrastructure (V2I) links. Fast channel variations high mobility environments preclude possibility of collecting accurate instantaneous state information at base station for centralized resource management. In response, we model as a learning problem, which is then solved using fingerprint-based deep Q-network method that amenable to distributed implementation. The V2V links, each acting an agent, collectively interact with communication environment, receive distinctive observations yet common reward, and learn improve power allocation through updating Q-networks gained experiences. abstract: We trained a large, deep convolutional neural network to classify the 1.2 million high-resolution images in ImageNet LSVRC-2010 contest into 1000 different classes. The network, has 60 parameters 650,000 neurons, consists five layers, some are followed by max-pooling three fully connected layers with final 1000-way softmax. title: ShuffleNet: An Extremely Efficient Convolutional Neural Network for Mobile Devices
abstract: We introduce an extremely computation-efficient CNN architecture named ShuffleNet, which is designed specially for mobile devices with very limited computing power (e.g., 10-150 MFLOPs). Experiments on ImageNet classification MS COCO object detection demonstrate the superior performance of ShuffleNet over other structures, e.g. lower top-1 error (absolute 7.8%) than recent MobileNet [12] task, under budget 40 MFLOPs. title: Quantized Neural Networks: Training Neural Networks with Low Precision Weights and Activations
abstract: We introduce a method to train Quantized Neural Networks (QNNs) --- neural networks with extremely low precision (e.g., 1-bit) weights and activations, at run-time. At train-time the quantized activations are used for computing parameter gradients. During forward pass, QNNs drastically reduce memory size accesses, replace most arithmetic operations bit-wise operations. abstract: Training a deep convolutional neural network (CNN) from scratch is difficult because it requires large amount of labeled training data and great deal expertise to ensure proper convergence. title: QRobot - a multitasking PC based robot control system
abstract: We describe QRobot, a low level real-time PC-based system for controlling PUMA robot manipulators. Specifically, we illustrate how standard controller can be retrofitted with interface boards that allow it to connected PC. All computational units are removed from the existing controller, and PC assumes role of computing control strategy. A is implemented 560 manipulator. The software then two high environment purposes trajectory generation, kinematics, inverse etc. Excellent tracking results obtained both systems. Some limitations environments were discovered during development QRobot. These provide motivation future new object oriented system.

title: Vision-Controlled Micro Flying Robots: From System Design to Autonomous Navigation and Mapping in GPS-Denied Environments
abstract: Autonomous microhelicopters will soon play a major role in tasks like search and rescue, environment monitoring, security surveillance, inspection. If they are further realized small scale, can also be used narrow outdoor indoor environments represent only limited risk for people. However, such operations, navigating based on global positioning system (GPS) information is not sufficient. Fully autonomous operation cities or other dense requires to fly at low altitudes, where GPS signals often shadowed, indoors actively explore unknown while avoiding collisions creating maps. This involves number of challenges all levels helicopter design, perception, actuation, control, navigation, which still have solved. The Swarm Micro Flying Robots (SFLY) project was European Union-funded with the goal swarm vision-controlled microaerial vehicles (MAVs) capable three-dimensional (3-D) mapping, optimal surveillance coverage GPS-denied environments. SFLY MAVs do rely remote radio beacons, motion-capture systems but by themselves using single onboard camera an inertial measurement unit (IMU). article describes technical that been faced results achieved from hardware design embedded programming vision-based navigation overview how modules work integrated into final system. Code, data sets, videos publicly available robotics community. Experimental demonstrating three autonomously performing 3-D mapping presented.

title: Design and control of a soft and continuously deformable 2D robotic manipulation system
abstract: In this paper we describe the design, fabrication, control, and experimental validation of a soft highly compliant 2D manipulator. We use cascaded PI PID computation drive cylinders to provide closed-loop control curvature for each segment. Furthermore, develop algorithms compute arm's forward inverse kinematics in manner consistent with piece-wise constant continuum manipulators. These systems enable robot autonomously follow trajectories. Experimental results consisting six show that controlled movement manipulator feasible.

title: Compact Ion-Trap Quantum Computing Demonstrator
abstract: Quantum information processing is steadily progressing from a purely academic discipline towards applications throughout science and industry. Transitioning lab-based, proof-of-concept experiments to robust, integrated realizations of quantum hardware an important step in this process. However, the nature traditional laboratory setups does not offer itself readily scaling up system sizes or allow for outside laboratory-grade environments. This transition requires overcoming challenges engineering integration without sacrificing state-of-the-art performance implementations. Here, we present 19-inch rack computing demonstrator based on $^{40}\textrm{Ca}^+$ optical qubits linear Paul trap address many these challenges. We outline mechanical, optical, electrical subsystems. Further, describe automation remote access components stack. conclude by describing characterization measurements relevant digital including entangling operations mediated Molmer-Sorenson interaction. title: Surface code quantum computing by lattice surgery
abstract: Abstract In recent years, surface codes have become a leading method for quantum error correction in theoretical large-scale computational and communications architecture designs. Their comparatively high fault-tolerant thresholds their natural two-dimensional nearest-neighbour (2DNN) structure make them an obvious choice large scale designs experimentally realistic systems. While fundamentally based on the toric code of Kitaev, there are many variants, two which planar- defect-based codes. Planar require fewer qubits to implement (for same strength correction), but restricted encoding single qubit information. Interactions between encoded achieved via transversal operations, thus destroying inherent 2DNN nature code. this paper we introduce new technique enabling coupling planar without maintaining computer. Our lattice surgery comprises splitting merging surfaces, enables us perform universal computation (including magic state injection) while removing need braided logic strictly design, hence reduces overall resources operations. Those further reduced by use rotated encoding. We show how allows distribute GHZ states more direct (and overhead friendly) manner, demonstration CNOT distance-3 logical is possible with 53 physical qubits, half that required any other known construction 2D.

title: Surface code quantum computing with error rates over 1%
abstract:
Large-scale quantum computation will only be achieved if experimentally
implementable error correction procedures are devised that can tolerate
experimentally achievable rates. We describe a correction
procedure requires 2-D square lattice of qubits interact
with their nearest neighbors, yet tolerate gate rates over
1%. The precise maximum tolerable rate depends on the model, and we
calculate values in range 1.1--1.4% for various physically reasonable
models. Even lowest value represents highest threshold rate
calculated to date geometrically constrained setting, 50%
improvement over previous record.

abstract:

abstract: Abstract Quantum computing aims at exploiting quantum phenomena to efficiently perform computations that are unfeasible even for the most powerful classical supercomputers. Among promising technological approaches, photonic offers advantages of low decoherence, information processing with modest cryogenic requirements, and native integration networks. So far, demonstrations light have implemented specific tasks specialized hardware, notably Gaussian boson sampling, which permits computational advantage be realized. Here we report a cloud-accessible versatile prototype based on single photons. The device comprises high-efficiency quantum-dot single-photon source feeding universal linear optical network reconfigurable chip hardware errors compensated by machine-learned transpilation process. Our full software stack allows remote control via logic gates or direct operations. For gate-based computation, benchmark one-, two- three-qubit state-of-the art fidelities 99.6 ± 0.1%, 93.8 0.6% 86 1.2%, respectively. We also implement variational eigensolver, use calculate energy levels hydrogen molecule chemical accuracy. photon classifier algorithm using three-photon-based neural six-photon sampling demonstration integrated circuit. Finally, heralded three-photon entanglement generation, key milestone toward measurement-based computing.

title: Quantum chaos border for quantum computing
abstract: We study a generic model of quantum computer, composed many qubits coupled by short-range interaction. Above critical interqubit coupling strength, chaos sets in, leading to ergodicity the computer eigenstates. In this regime noninteracting qubit structure disappears, eigenstates become complex, and operability is destroyed. Despite fact that spacing between multiqubit states drops exponentially with number n, we show border decreases only linearly n. This opens broad parameter region where efficient operation remains possible.

for fault-tolerant, concatenation of

title: Experimental Quantum Computing to Solve Systems of Linear Equations
abstract: Solving linear systems of equations is ubiquitous in all areas science and engineering. With rapidly growing data sets, such a task can be intractable for classical computers, as the best known algorithms require time proportional to number variables N. A recently proposed quantum algorithm shows that computers could solve scale order log(N), giving an exponential speedup over computers. Here we realize simplest instance this algorithm, solving 2×2 various input vectors on computer. We use four bits controlled logic gates implement every subroutine required, demonstrating working principle algorithm.

title: Quantum algorithms for algebraic problems
abstract:
Quantum computers can execute algorithms that dramatically outperform
classical computation. As the best-known example, Shor discovered an efficient
quantum algorithm for factoring integers, whereas appears to be
difficult classical computers. Understanding what other computational
problems be solved significantly faster using quantum is one of
the major challenges in theory of computation, and such algorithms
motivate formidable task building a large-scale computer. This
article reviews current state algorithms, focusing on algorithms
with superpolynomial speedup over particular, on
problems with algebraic flavor.

title: Quantum Algorithm for Systems of Linear Equations with Exponentially Improved Dependence on Precision
abstract:
Harrow, Hassidim, and Lloyd showed that for a suitably specified $N \times N$
matrix $A$ $N$-dimensional vector $\vec{b}$, there is quantum algorithm
that outputs state proportional to the solution of linear system
of equations $A\vec{x}=\vec{b}$. If sparse well-conditioned, their
algorithm runs in time $\mathrm{poly}(\log N, 1/\epsilon)$, where $\epsilon$ is
the desired precision output state. We improve this an algorithm
whose running polynomial $\log(1/\epsilon)$, exponentially improving
the dependence on while keeping essentially same on
other parameters. Our algorithm based general technique for
implementing any operator with suitable Fourier or Chebyshev series
representation. This allows us bypass phase estimation
algorithm, whose prohibitive.

To address this discrepancy, a quantum-classical hybrid optimization scheme known as 'the variational eigensolver' was developed (Peruzzo et al 2014 Nat. title: Shor's discrete logarithm quantum algorithm for elliptic curves
abstract: We show in some detail how to implement Shor's efficient quantum algorithm for discrete logarithms the particular case of elliptic curve groups. It turns out that this problem a smaller computer can solve problems further beyond current computing than integer factorisation. A 160 bit cryptographic key could be broken on using around 1000 qubits while factoring security-wise equivalent 1024 RSA modulus would require about 2000 qubits. In paper we only consider curves over GF(p) and not yet equally important ones GF(2^n) or other finite fields. The main technical difficulty is Euclid's gcd compute multiplicative inverses modulo p. As runtime depends input, one encountered ``quantum halting problem''.

title: Polynomial-time quantum algorithm for the simulation of chemical dynamics
abstract: The computational cost of exact methods for quantum simulation using classical computers grows exponentially with system size. As a consequence, these techniques can be applied only to small systems. By contrast, we demonstrate that could exactly simulate chemical reactions in polynomial time. Our algorithm uses the split-operator approach and explicitly simulates all electron-nuclear interelectronic interactions quadratic Surprisingly, this treatment is not more accurate than Born–Oppenheimer approximation but faster efficient as well, about four atoms. Although preparation measurement arbitrary states computer inefficient, here how prepare interest efficiently. We also show efficiently obtain chemically relevant observables, such state-to-state transition probabilities thermal reaction rates. Quantum outperform current 100 qubits.

title: Learning the quantum algorithm for state overlap
abstract: Short-depth algorithms are crucial for reducing computational error on near-term quantum computers, which decoherence and gate infidelity remain important issues. Here we present a machine-learning approach discovering such algorithms. We apply our method to ubiquitous primitive: computing the overlap $\\mathrm{Tr}(\\rho \\sigma )$ between two states ρ σ. The standard algorithm this task, known as Swap Test, is used in many applications support vector machines, and, when specialized = σ, quantifies Renyi entanglement. Here, find that have shorter depths than including one has constant depth (independent of problem size). Furthermore, hardware-specific connectivity sets by Rigetti's IBM's computers demonstrate derive significantly reduce error—compared Test—on these computers.

abstract: A quantum system will stay near its instantaneous ground state if the Hamiltonian that governs evolution varies slowly enough. This adiabatic behavior is basis of a new class algorithms for computing. We tested one such algorithm by applying it to randomly generated hard instances an NP-complete problem. title: q-means: A quantum algorithm for unsupervised machine learning
abstract: Quantum machine learning is one of the most promising applications a full-scale quantum computer. Both times are only polylogarithmic datapoints $N$. Our provides substantial savings compared $k$-means runs $O(kdN)$ particularly case large datasets.

title: Experimental realization of quantum algorithm for solving linear systems of equations
abstract:
Quantum computers have the potential of solving certain problems
exponentially faster than classical computers. Recently, Harrow, Hassidim and
Lloyd proposed a quantum algorithm for linear systems equations:
given an $N\times{N}$ matrix $A$ and vector $\vec b$, find $\vec
x$ that satisfies $A\vec x = \vec b$. It has been shown using the
algorithm one could obtain solution encoded in state $|x$ using
$O(\log{N})$ operations, while algorithms require at least
O(N) steps. If is not interested $\vec{x}$ itself but
certain statistical feature ${x}|M|x$ ($M$ some quantum
mechanical operator), will be able to achieve exponential
speedup over best as $N$ grows. Here we report a
proof-of-concept experimental demonstration a
4-qubit nuclear magnetic resonance (NMR) information processor. For all
the three sets experiments with different choices the
solutions 96% fidelity. This experiment first implementation of
the algorithm. Because common problem nearly all
fields science engineering, also discuss implication our
results on practical
linear systems.

title: Quantum algorithm for association rules mining
abstract:
Association rules mining (ARM) is one of the most important problems in
knowledge discovery and data mining. Given a transaction database that has a
large number transactions items, task ARM to acquire
consumption habits customers by discovering relationships between
itemsets (sets items). In this paper, we address in quantum settings
and propose algorithm for key part ARM, finding out frequent
itemsets from candidate itemsets acquiring their supports.
Specifically, case which there are $M_f^{(k)}$ frequent $k$-itemsets
in $M_c^{(k)}$ $k$-itemsets ($M_f^{(k)} \leq M_c^{(k)}$), our
algorithm can efficiently mine these estimate their
supports using parallel amplitude estimation amplification
with complexity $\mathcal{O}(\frac{k\sqrt{M_c^{(k)}M_f^{(k)}}}{\epsilon})$,
where $\epsilon$ error estimating supports. Compared with the
classical counterpart, classical sampling-based algorithm, whose is
$\mathcal{O}(\frac{kM_c^{(k)}}{\epsilon^2})$, our algorithm
quadratically improves dependence on both the
best when $M_f^{(k)}\ll M_c^{(k)}$ alone worst
case $M_f^{(k)}\approx M_c^{(k)}$.

title: Holographic quantum algorithms for simulating correlated spin systems
abstract:
We present a suite of "holographic" quantum algorithms for efficient
ground-state preparation and dynamical evolution correlated spin-systems,
which require far-fewer qubits than the number spins being simulated. The
algorithms exploit equivalence between matrix-product states (MPS) and
quantum channels, along with partial measurement qubit re-use, in order to
simulate $D$-dimensional spin system using only ($D$-1)-dimensional subset
of an ancillary register whose size scales
logarithmically amount entanglement simulated state.
Ground can either be directly prepared from known MPS representation,
or obtained via holographic variational eigensolver (holoVQE).
Dynamics under local Hamiltonians time $t$ also simulated
with additional (multiplicative) ${\rm poly}(t)$ overhead qubit
resources. These techniques open door to efficient simulation of
MPS exponentially large bond-dimension, including ground-states 2D and
3D systems, or thermalizing dynamics rapid growth. As a
demonstration potential resource savings, we implement holoVQE
simulation antiferromagnetic Heisenberg chain on trapped-ion quantum
computer, achieving within $10(3)\%$ exact ground-state energy an
infinite pair qubits.

abstract: We propose a quantum algorithm for support matrix machines (SMMs) that efficiently addresses an image classification problem by introducing least-squares reformulation. This consists of two core subroutines: inversion (Harrow-Hassidim-Lloyd, HHL) and singular value thresholding (QSVT) algorithm. The algorithms can be implemented on universal computer with complexity $O\left[log\left(npq\right)\right]$ $O\left[log\left(pq\right)\right]$, respectively, where $n$ is the number training data $pq$ size feature space. By iterating algorithms, we find parameters SMM classfication model. Our analysis shows both HHL QSVT achieve exponential increase speed over their classical counterparts.

title: Quantum-optimal-control-inspired ansatz for variational quantum algorithms
abstract:

title: Adiabatic quantum algorithms as quantum phase transitions: First versus second order
abstract:
In the continuum limit (large number of qubits), adiabatic quantum algorithms
display a remarkable similarity to sweeps through phase transitions. We
find that transitions second or higher order are advantageous in comparison
to those first order. With this insight, we propose novel adiabatic
quantum algorithm for solution 3-satisfiability (3-SAT) problems (exact
cover), which is significantly faster than previous proposals according to
numerical simulations (up 20 qubits). These findings suggest algorithms can solve NP-complete such as 3-SAT much faster
than Grover search routine (yielding quadratic enhancement), possibly
even with an exponential speed-up. PACS: 03.67.-a, 03.67.Lx, 73.43.Nq,
64.70.-p.

title: Quantum State Transfer and Entanglement Distribution among Distant Nodes in a Quantum Network
abstract:
We propose a scheme to utilize photons for ideal quantum transmission between
atoms located at spatially-separated nodes of network. The
transmission protocol employs special laser pulses which excite an atom inside
an optical cavity the sending node so that its state is mapped into a
time-symmetric photon wavepacket will enter receiving node
and be absorbed by there with unit probability. Implementation our

title: Distributed authentication in Kerberos using public key cryptography
abstract: The authors describe a method for fully distributed authentication using public key cryptography within the Kerberos ticket framework. By distributing most of workload away from trusted intermediary and to communicating parties, significant enhancements security scalability can be achieved as compared V5. Privacy clients is also enhanced. A working implementation this extended protocol has been developed, migration plan proposed transition traditional based Kerberos.

title: Research on Certificateless Public Key Cryptography
abstract: PDF HTML阅读 XML下载 导出引用 引用提醒 无证书公钥密码体制研究 DOI: 10.3724/SP.J.1001.2011.04007 作者: 作者单位: 作者简介: 通讯作者: 中图分类号: 基金项目: 国家自然科学基金(60673070); 江苏省自然科学基金(BK2006217); 南京工业大学青年教师学术基金(39704023) Research on Certificateless Public Key Cryptography Author: Affiliation: Fund Project: 摘要 | 图/表 访问统计 参考文献 相似文献 引证文献 资源附件 文章评论 摘要:无证书公钥密码体制(certificateless public key cryptography,简称CL-PKC)是在基于身份的公钥密码体制(identity-based cryptography,简称ID-PKC)的基础上提出来的一种新型公钥密码体制,没有密钥托管问题、不需要使用公钥证书,使得无证书公钥密码体制从其概念提出的初始就受到了学术界和工业界的极大关注.从2003 年至今,它一直是密码学和信息安全领域非常活跃的研究热点.其理论和技术在不断地丰富和发展.到目前为止,已经积累了大量的研究成果.将对这些成果进行较为系统的整理、分析、比较和简要的评述,并探讨该领域研究尚存在的不足及值得进一步研究的问题. Abstract:Certificateless cryptography (CL-PKC for short) is a new type of cryptography, which developed the foundation identity based (ID-PKC short). CL-PKC elimilates escrow problem and need certificate. These two advantages are what makes it attractive to research community industrial world form beginning its birth. It has been very active hot topic in field cryptology information security. In about seven years, study advanced step by step, making theories techniques more enriching. This paper revisits, analyzes, compares, briefly reviews some main results. Furthermore, this discusses existing problems that deserve further investigation.
authors: Futai Zhang, Yinxia Sun, Lei Zhang, Manman Geng, Sujuan Li
affiliations: Nanjing Normal University, Nanjing Tech University; Nanjing Normal University; Universitat Rovira i Virgili; Nanjing Normal University; Nanjing Normal University, Nanjing Tech University

title: A light-weight certificate-less public key cryptography scheme based on ECC
abstract: With the rapid development of mobile computing, more and devices, such as smart phones tablets are able to access Internet. As these devices usually battery powered, energy efficiency is a very important issue. For most applications, saving should be considered at design stage. title: BRAPH: A graph theory software for the analysis of brain connectivity
abstract: The brain is a large-scale complex network whose workings rely on the interaction between its various regions. In past few years, organization of human has been studied extensively using concepts from graph theory, where represented as set nodes connected by edges. This representation connectome can be used to assess important measures that reflect topological architecture. We have developed freeware MatLab-based software (BRAPH-BRain Analysis graPH theory) for connectivity analysis networks derived structural magnetic resonance imaging (MRI), functional MRI (fMRI), positron emission tomography (PET) and electroencephalogram (EEG) data. BRAPH allows building matrices, calculating global local measures, performing non-parametric permutations group comparisons, assessing modules in network, comparing results random networks. By contrast other toolboxes, it longitudinal comparisons same patients across different points time. Furthermore, even though user-friendly interface provided, architecture program modular (object-oriented) so easily expanded customized. To demonstrate abilities BRAPH, we performed theory analyses two separate studies. first study, data, assessed differences nodal topology healthy controls, with amnestic mild cognitive impairment, Alzheimer's disease. second resting-state fMRI compared controls Parkinson's impairment.

title: Functional neural network analysis in frontotemporal dementia and Alzheimer's disease using EEG and graph theory
abstract: With decreasing local and global connectivity parameters, the large-scale functional brain network organization in AD deviates from optimal 'small-world' structure towards a more 'random' type. This is associated with less efficient information exchange between areas, supporting disconnection hypothesis of AD. Surprisingly, FTLD patients show changes opposite direction, (perhaps excessively) 'ordered' structure, possibly reflecting different underlying pathophysiological process.

Based observation texel fetches greatly impact off-chip traffic, we propose two architectural designs enable Processing-In-Memory based efficient rendering. title: Learning Indoor Inverse Rendering with 3D Spatially-Varying Lighting
abstract: In this work, we address the problem of jointly estimating albedo, normals, depth and 3D spatially-varying lighting from a single image. Most existing methods formulate task as image-to-image translation, ignoring properties scene. However, indoor scenes contain complex light transport where 2D representation is insufficient. paper, propose unified, learning-based inverse rendering framework that formulates lighting. Inspired by classic volume techniques, novel Volumetric Spherical Gaussian for lighting, which parameterizes exitant radiance scene surfaces on voxel grid. We design physics-based differentiable renderer utilizes our representation, energy-conserving image formation process enables joint training all intrinsic with re-rendering constraint. Our model ensures physically correct predictions avoids need ground-truth HDR not easily accessible. Experiments show method outperforms prior works both quantitatively qualitatively, capable producing photorealistic results AR applications such virtual object insertion even highly specular objects.

title: 3D rendering of shipwrecks from synthetic aperture sonar
abstract: Synthetic aperture sonar (SAS) bathymetric mapping provides centimeter-scale horizontal resolution of the seafloor. Typically, SAS data has been visualized as 2D images or gridded 2.5D surfaces, but it can also be plotted 3D point clouds. Even if imaging system is not fully volumetric, geometry leaves possibility having points that reside in same lateral positions a ground coordinate system. Visualizing these without geometric distortion loss challenging. In this paper we investigate use full-fledged rendering with artificial lighting and shading techniques order to convey more available information. Hasil pengujiannya akan membantu setiap CG Artist atau pengguna aplikasi dalam memilih engine cocok untuk karya 3D Artwork mereka kerjakan. Teknik dianalisis yaitu merupakan jenis pre-rendered proses rendernya bisa menghabiskan waktu lama karena pemrosesannya rumit, kemudian real-time render-nya sendiri tidak begitu membutuhkan lama, prosesnya dilakukan secara realtime. Vertices dan Texture digunakan sebagai parameter pengukuran pengujian kedua rendering. Analisis menghasilkan kecepatan render, ukuran file hasil kualitas gambar dari render. penelitian mampu menyatakan bahwa mengungguli rendering, pada output jauh berbeda file-nya, lebih unggul dibandingkan Cycles.

abstract: Cacao (Theobroma cacao) is a crop of the humid lowland tropics produced largely by small-scale producers and often on farms with canopy shade trees. abstract:
